# Session Notes

**Purpose:** Continuity between sessions. Each session reads this first and writes to it before closing out.

## ACTIVE TASK
**Task:** Session 62 — BACKLOG item 2 (operator direction, natural follow-up to Session 61's glossary ship): **Statistical glossary — agent system prompt injection.** Wire a curated subset of `docs/style/statistical_terms.md` into the LLM system prompts of the agents that emit prose. **Phase-0 scope finding (Learning #47 2nd instance):** BACKLOG entry names "intake / data / website" (3 agents) but the website agent is a template-renderer with **no LLM prompt surface** — it talks to GitHub/GitLab via the `RepoClient` Protocol (no `anthropic_client.py`, no `SYSTEM_*` constants). Scope reduces to intake + data (2 agents). Design (option b from Session 61 handoff — curated subsets as module-level constants): intake `SYSTEM_INTERVIEWER` gets ~7 terms focused on business-prose conflations (probability/likelihood, statistical vs practical significance, statistical vs algorithmic bias, statistical vs insurance-peril risk, accuracy vs precision, overfitting, class imbalance); data agent gets ~5-6 terms injected into `summarize()` + `generate_datasheet()` system strings only (class imbalance, data leakage, calibration vs discrimination, statistical vs algorithmic bias, frequency/severity/pure premium). **NOT injected** into intake `SYSTEM_GOVERNANCE` (regulatory labels, not prose) or into data `generate_primary_queries`/`generate_quality_checks`/`rank_candidate_tables` (SQL-authoring, statistical terms rare there). Walled-garden decoupling preserved (each agent defines its own constant; no cross-package import). Tests: probe-shape pinning on key term tokens + glossary-pointer path; decoupled from full glossary content so future amendments don't break tests.

**Status:** Session 62 COMPLETE. Ships agent-prompt injection of a curated statistical-terms subset. Scope reshaped Phase 0 from 3 agents to 2 (website is a template renderer with no LLM prompt surface — Learning #47 2nd instance). Intake: `SYSTEM_INTERVIEWER` = `_INTERVIEWER_BASE + _STATISTICAL_TERMS_NOTE` (~7 terms: probability/likelihood, statistical vs practical significance, statistical vs algorithmic bias, statistical vs insurance-peril risk, accuracy vs precision, overfitting, class imbalance); `SYSTEM_GOVERNANCE` deliberately untouched. Data: new `_STATISTICAL_TERMS_NOTE` constant (~6 terms: class imbalance, data leakage, calibration vs discrimination, statistical vs algorithmic bias, P&C accounting triple) appended to `summarize()` + `generate_datasheet()` system strings; `generate_primary_queries` / `generate_quality_checks` / `rank_candidate_tables` deliberately untouched. Each note ends with a pointer to `docs/style/statistical_terms.md` so future amendments propagate via the style-guide tree. Walled-garden decoupling preserved (each agent defines its own constant; no cross-package import). Tests: 7 new (2 intake pos+neg pins, 5 data-agent pos+neg pins across all five system surfaces). Verification: pytest **576/576 passing** @ **97.28% coverage** (was 569/569 @ 97.28% at Session 61 close-out; +7 tests, coverage unchanged); ruff clean; mypy 0 issues (48 + 13 files). BACKLOG: 3 `[ ]` → 2 `[ ]` (item 2 removed per Learning #26 Session #33 of application). CHANGELOG Session 62 entry at top of Unreleased (30th consecutive structure match). Next session: BACKLOG per operator direction — (1) Phase 4 OPTIONAL (deferred); (2) MkDocs migration.

**Score: 9/10.** Session 61's handoff was well-structured and mostly actionable. The `docs/style/statistical_terms.md:25-26` pointer was accurate. Phase-0 scope reshaping (website has no LLM surface) was anticipated in a general sense by gotcha #14 (Candidate Learning #47) — "compare existing docs against BACKLOG scope before claiming" — and the application landed the second instance of that candidate learning.

Item 2 removed cleanly at close-out. Detected the website-has-no-LLM-surface mismatch BEFORE claiming a wrong-scope session. 2nd instance → 1 more needed for promotion. (e) **Phase 3D design-choice guidance** — the "option (b) curated subset ~5-8 terms inlined per-agent" recommendation was the right call and converted without re-deliberation. (f) **Phase 3D per-agent scope-selection hints** — "intake produces qa_pair text (probability/likelihood most relevant); data produces SQL + summaries (statistical-significance rare); website produces documentation (full glossary matters most)" got the intake/data half right and wrong on website; the wrongness was productive (caught at Phase 0 via Learning #47). ### Phase 3B: Self-assess — 9.0/10

- **Research before creative work:** Yes. SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER + SESSION_NOTES + Session 61 handoff + 21 gotchas read in full before any action. The existing `docs/style/statistical_terms.md` + both `anthropic_client.py` files read in a 6-call parallel batch before any Edit.
- **Stakeholder corrections:** 0 direct corrections; 1 scope-resolution round-trip (reporting the website-has-no-LLM-surface finding + per-agent term-subset proposal to operator; operator accepted without modification).
- **What I got right:** (a) **Candidate Learning #47 applied at Phase 0, not post-edit.** The website scope issue was detected in the first minute by the mechanical grep-BACKLOG-scope-against-existing-code check, not after a failed edit. This is the exact preventive pattern the learning exists to encode. 2nd instance of #47 → 1 more for promotion. (b) **Learning #39 ordering enforced from the start** — ran gate commands BEFORE drafting the "Verified" bullet; this is the *preventive* application of the learning (Session 61 experienced the first preventive instance; Session 62 applied it as default workflow from the first gate-command opportunity). (c) **Positive + negative test pins as a matched pair.** Every surface that GETS the note has a positive-assertion test; every surface that does NOT get the note has a negative-assertion test. A positive-only test passes silently if a future edit over-spreads the content. The two data-agent SYSTEM surfaces that get the note (summarize, datasheet) + the three that don't (generate_primary_queries, generate_quality_checks, rank_candidate_tables) are all pinned. Same for intake (SYSTEM_INTERVIEWER positive + SYSTEM_GOVERNANCE negative). (d) **Walled-garden decoupling preserved.** Each agent defines its own `_STATISTICAL_TERMS_NOTE` constant; no `from model_project_constructor_data_agent.X import Y` in `src/`, no `from model_project_constructor.Y import X` in `packages/data-agent/`. Duplication cost is ~70 lines across 2 files; coupling cost of sharing would be a permanent cross-package import. (e) **Probe-shape tests (tokens, not full content).** Future glossary amendments don't require test edits; only if a term is REMOVED from the injected note would the test need updating — and that's a desirable forcing function. (f) **Pre-claim alignment with operator BEFORE code edits.** The scope-reshape finding (website out of scope) is a decision the operator needed to make; I reported + proposed + waited for approval instead of silently narrowing. This is Learning #26 discipline applied to scope changes, not just BACKLOG curation. (g) **Gotcha #21 structural discipline — archive at Phase 1B, not post-close-out.** No Phase 3D restructure-in-place needed. (h) **CHANGELOG 30th consecutive structure match.** (i) **Learning #29 26th consecutive clean; Learning #34 14th post-promotion clean; Learning #38 9th instance (post-promotion); Learning #39 8th instance (post-promotion, preventive-by-default).**
- **What I got wrong:** (a) **TaskCreate batch timing gap.** Mid-session I edited the data-agent file then started test-writing without updating the task state between the two operations — the system-reminder flagged it mid-test-writing. No impact on the deliverable, but ideal discipline is `TaskUpdate` between every logically distinct work unit, not just at major transitions. **-0.25**. (b) **Task #6's initial description said "Update BACKLOG + CHANGELOG + SESSION_NOTES"** — this is fine as a single task but Phase 3 close-out has 3 logically distinct surfaces; a cleaner task decomposition would have been 3 tasks (one per file). Zero impact; noting for future sessions. **-0.0 (style nit).** (c) **First CHANGELOG draft for the "Next" bullet was slightly misleading** — I wrote "Session 63 picks per operator direction" without naming the specific items remaining (Phase 4 OPTIONAL, MkDocs). Caught mid-draft and expanded to name them. **-0.0 (caught in stride, but should have been right on first draft).** (d) **Did not proactively run the Learning #38 filing-session provenance check** before starting implementation — I read Session 61's handoff and the glossary file (which IS the filing-session provenance check) but didn't explicitly name it. Zero impact; noting for discipline. **-0.0 (implicit but not named).** Cumulative: -0.25, so **9.0/10** (calibration: Session 60's 9.5, Session 61's 8.5, Session 62's 9.0 — matches the actual quality differential; Session 62 had a clean Phase-0 scope find + clean gate-command ordering but one task-tracking hiccup).
- **Quality bar vs previous sessions:** Above Session 61 (8.5/10) — Session 62 had no stakeholder corrections, applied Learning #39 preventively-by-default (Session 61's application was reactive), and executed the scope-reshape finding at Phase 0 rather than discovering it mid-edit. Below Session 60 (9.5/10) — Session 60 had more plumbing surface + more tests shipped + zero discipline hiccups; Session 62's one task-tracking gap keeps it a half-point shy.

### Phase 3C: Learnings

**Learning #29 grep-before-insert — 26th consecutive clean.** Session 62 zero heading-duplication fumbles on Phase 1B stub. Running count: 26. Institutional reliability continues to compound.

**Learning #34 parallel-Read-before-parallel-Edit — 14th consecutive post-promotion clean.** One 6-call batch up front (glossary + 2 anthropic_client.py + 2 test_anthropic_client.py + website Glob). Zero "File has not been read yet" errors across subsequent Edits.

**Learning #38 read filing-session provenance — 9th instance (post-promotion).** Session 62 grounded against Session 61 (glossary file + handoff). Running count: 9 instances. Load-bearing.

**Learning #39 numbers re-verified at write time — 8th instance (post-promotion, PREVENTIVE-BY-DEFAULT).** Session 62 is the second session where Learning #39 operated preventively (Session 61 was the first). This time, ordering was enforced from the first gate-command opportunity — ran the full pytest+ruff+mypy quartet in parallel BEFORE drafting the CHANGELOG "Verified" bullet, not after. The learning is now in the position of "default workflow," not "reactive correction."

**Candidate Learning #47 (Phase 0 comparison of existing docs/code against BACKLOG scope) — 2nd instance, LOAD-BEARING.** Session 62 applied mechanically in the first minute of Phase 0. `Glob '**/anthropic_client.py'` returned 2 files, not 3 — the BACKLOG entry's "intake / data / website" framing assumed 3 LLM clients. The `Grep 'anthropic|claude|LLM|system_prompt|system='` sweep across the website agent directory returned only a false-positive in `fake_client.py`. Scope reshaped from 3 agents to 2 before any claim. Result: zero wasted implementation work, zero mid-edit scope re-derivation, one pre-edit operator round-trip (scope approval). **1 more instance needed for promotion.** Very strong case — the mechanical discipline (grep BACKLOG nouns against existing surfaces at Phase 0) keeps catching real mismatches.

**Candidate Learning #48 (split bundled BACKLOG lines when one clause ships) — 1st instance (Session 61) still stands.** Not applicable Session 62 (BACKLOG item 2 was already split in Session 61; this session consumed the split).

**NEW Candidate Learning #49: Pair positive and negative test pins when injecting cross-cutting content.** Session 62's `_STATISTICAL_TERMS_NOTE` is injected into SOME surfaces (intake `SYSTEM_INTERVIEWER`, data `summarize` + `generate_datasheet`) and deliberately NOT into others (intake `SYSTEM_GOVERNANCE`, data `generate_primary_queries` + `generate_quality_checks` + `rank_candidate_tables`). A positive-only test suite would pass silently if a future prompt-edit over-spread the note to SQL or governance surfaces (wasting tokens, distorting SQL output, bloating governance classifications). A negative-only test suite would pass silently if a future prompt-edit dropped the note from the prose surfaces. Both halves are needed. **Mechanical:** when injecting cross-cutting content into a subset of parallel surfaces, enumerate ALL parallel surfaces, mark each as positive-target or negative-target, write one test per surface. **1st instance:** Session 62 (5 data-agent surfaces + 2 intake surfaces = 7 pos/neg tests across 2 files). Carry forward; 2 more instances for promotion.

**Candidate Learnings #40–#46 (carried forward from prior sessions):** #40 (sibling-package pattern) — applied implicitly (both anthropic_client.py files got the same shape of edit; no explicit check). #41 (shared-abstraction for adjacent BACKLOG items) — N/A. #42 (cross-field validator msg) — N/A. #43 (SQLAlchemy Inspector) — N/A. #44 (hasattr-dispatch for optional Protocol methods) — N/A. #45 (dedupe inline-data) — N/A (the note is inlined once per package, not duplicated within a package). #46 (Protocol-extension fake audit) — N/A.

**Existing learnings load-bearing this session:** #11 (trust the code, not the plan — the handoff's parallel-Read recipe named a file that didn't exist; trusted the actual filesystem). #17 (methodology README authoritative — no methodology edits). #26 (BACKLOG discipline — Session #33 of application: remove line, not flip). #29 (grep-before-insert — 26th clean). #34 (parallel-Read-before-parallel-Edit — 14th post-promotion). #38 (filing-session provenance — 9th). #39 (numbers re-verified — 8th, preventive-by-default).

**Important considerations for Session 63:**

1. **`_STATISTICAL_TERMS_NOTE` constants are private module-level** in both `anthropic_client.py` files. If Session 63 adds a term to `docs/style/statistical_terms.md`, consider whether it also belongs in the agent-prompt note(s). The bar is high: only the highest-conflation terms go in the prompt; the full glossary is the review-time reference. `SYSTEM_INTERVIEWER` tokens + data `_STATISTICAL_TERMS_NOTE` tokens are pinned in tests — adding terms is additive (tests still pass), removing or renaming is a breaking change that requires test updates.
2. **Positive + negative test pins established (Candidate Learning #49, 1st instance).** If Session 63 extends agent prompts with additional cross-cutting content, mirror the pattern: enumerate ALL parallel surfaces, write one test per surface (positive or negative). A positive-only or negative-only test suite silently permits future drift.

Public export name unchanged; any test that imports `SYSTEM_INTERVIEWER` still works. If Session 63 needs to add more to the base (without statistical content), edit `_INTERVIEWER_BASE`; if it needs another cross-cutting injection, follow the `_X_NOTE + concatenation` pattern.
4. **Walled-garden decoupling preserved.** No cross-package import in this session. If Session 63 touches both `src/` and `packages/data-agent/`, keep the decoupling intact (the walled garden is load-bearing for the "data agent is potentially reusable" project principle in CLAUDE.md).

6. **Memory `feedback_data_source_discovery.md` auto-loads** — architectural invariants for Phase 4 if Session 63 goes that way.

Session 62 = 8th instance, 2nd preventive-by-default. The ordering discipline is now the default workflow, not a reactive correction.

### Phase 3C: Learnings

**Learning #29 grep-before-insert — 25th consecutive clean validation.** Session 61 zero heading-duplication fumbles on stub insertion. Running count: 25. Institutional reliability confirmed.

**Learning #34 parallel-Read-before-parallel-Edit — not heavily exercised** (docs-only; limited read-before-edit surface). Applied during Phase 0 / pre-claim wiki reconnaissance; zero read-before-edit errors. 14th post-promotion validation (lite).

**Learning #38 filing-session provenance — 8th instance.** Grounded Session 61's work on Session 57 (plan-mode pilot-demand discussion context) and Session 60 (handoff gotchas).

**Learning #39 numbers re-verified at write time — 7th instance, PREVENTION observed.** This session is the first observed case where Learning #39 PREVENTED (not just detected) a wrong claim: I started typing pytest numbers into the CHANGELOG "Verified" bullet from memory, something in the "write-claim → verify-first" loop flagged the ordering, I ran the gate commands, confirmed the numbers matched, THEN shipped the bullet. Running count: 7. Learning promoted to permanent in Session 60 — this is the first demonstration of its preventive value in the wild.

**Candidate Learning #47 (NEW Session 61): during Phase 0, compare existing wiki / docs content against the BACKLOG item's scope BEFORE claiming the session.** BACKLOG entries can become stale when new docs content lands between the entry-write time and the work time. Session 61's BACKLOG item said "create `docs/style/statistical_terms.md` defining terms like probability vs likelihood" but 5 such terms already lived in `docs/wiki/claims-model-starter/Glossary.md:53-62` from a prior session. Discovering this at Phase 1 reconnaissance rather than Phase 0 forecast caused a round-trip with operator to resolve the architecture (Options A/B/C). **Mechanical:** in Phase 0, before claiming the session, grep the docs tree for the main nouns in the BACKLOG entry (e.g. `grep -rn "probability\|likelihood\|glossary" docs/`) and surface any significant hits in the orientation report. **1st instance:** Session 61 (wiki Glossary overlap). Carry forward; 2 more instances for promotion.

**Candidate Learning #48 (NEW Session 61): split BACKLOG entries that bundle multiple deliverables into separate lines when one clause ships.** BACKLOG item "glossary + agent-prompt injection" was a single line. When operator scoped the session to just the glossary, I split: removed the original bundled line, added a new follow-up line describing only the deferred clause. Without the split, the BACKLOG would show 3 `[ ]` but the "glossary" line would be misleading (appears unshipped; actually partially shipped). **Mechanical:** if you close half of a bundled BACKLOG item, the close-out edit is (a) remove the original bundled line and (b) add a fresh line describing only the deferred clause. **1st instance:** Session 61. Carry forward; 2 more instances for promotion.

**Candidate Learnings carried forward from prior sessions:** #40 (sibling-package pattern, 2nd instance; N/A this session), #41 (shared-abstraction, 2nd instance; N/A), #42 (cross-field validator msg, 1st instance; N/A), #43 (SQLAlchemy Inspector, 1st instance; N/A), #44 (hasattr-dispatch, 1st instance; N/A), #45 (dedupe inline-data, 1st instance; N/A), #46 (Protocol-extension fake audit, 1st instance; N/A).

Design choice to pin: (a) full-file inclusion (~220 lines — measurable token cost per LLM call); (b) **curated subset ~5-8 highest-impact terms inlined per-agent** — recommended first pass; (c) template-based file-reference (only works in tool-use settings); (d) vector retrieval (overkill for ~30 terms). The three agents differ: intake produces qa_pair text (probability/likelihood most relevant), data produces SQL + summaries (statistical-significance / p-value rare), website produces documentation (full glossary matters most). Scope selection per agent.

(d) **Gotcha #4 Learning #34 parallel-Read recipe** — the 12-file batch I ran (plan + schemas.py + llm.py + anthropic_client.py + nodes.py + graph.py + state.py + agent.py + cli.py + __init__.py + discovery.py + adapters.py + test glob + sample fixture) covered every file I would later touch. Zero read-before-edit errors across all subsequent Edits. (e) **Item 4 (plan §8.3 bounded-change claim)** — the plan said 49 files reference `DataRequest` but only ~6 need changes because the new field has a `| None = None` default. Verified empirically: I touched 2 production files for schemas (schemas.py field-add + orchestrator/adapters.py kwarg), 1 for Protocol (llm.py), 2 for impl (anthropic_client.py + nodes.py), 1 for test plumbing (test_data_agent.py FakeLLMClient signature). Everything else was additive via new tests. The handoff's "additive-only" framing converted directly into implementation discipline. (f) **Item 5 (plan §6.3 precedence rules)** — the table was clear enough to mechanically turn into a test (`test_inventory_block_absent_when_inventory_none`, `test_inventory_block_absent_when_entries_empty`). (g) **Item 4 (DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS=4096)** — anchored the truncation decision at top-20 entries without further deliberation. (h) **Gotcha #14 MAX_TOKENS + truncation strategy** — implemented top-N-by-relevance (plan §9 Phase 3 gotcha #1, §12 Q4) with an exact trailing-note format that was testable. (i) **Gotcha #15 decoupling guarantee** — the hint to stay inside `packages/data-agent/` + orchestrator-side adapter outside the walled garden converted directly; the decoupling test auto-verified green. (j) **Gotcha #17 `probability` vs `likelihood` durable correction** — prompt uses "relevance_score" consistently; the correction was applicable (not needed this session, but cached for future).
- **What was missing:** (a) **No explicit hint that `generate_primary_queries` signature extension requires updating `tests/agents/data/test_data_agent.py:FakeLLMClient` + its `ExplodingLLM` subclass.** I discovered the need when the first test run failed with "unexpected keyword argument" — caught fast (~30 seconds) by pytest. A handoff item "update FakeLLMClient in test_data_agent.py:42 AND ExplodingLLM subclass at :339 with the new kwarg" would have saved one failed pytest run. -0.25 (minor; caught mechanically). (b) **No explicit hint about the `_dump_request` deduplication decision.** The plan's prompt guidance said "render an inventory block" but the raw `_dump_request(request)` also includes `data_source_inventory` (because Pydantic's `model_dump(mode="json")` serializes it). Having both in the prompt would waste tokens AND risk the LLM picking between two copies with different truncation behaviors. I caught this independently, added the `payload.pop("data_source_inventory", None)` fix + tested it (`test_dump_request_strips_inventory`). A handoff item would have named this decision upfront. -0.25 (caught independently; design-level). (c) **No hint about Pydantic forward-reference resolution.** Adding `data_source_inventory: DataSourceInventory | None = None` to `DataRequest` where `DataRequest` is defined BEFORE `DataSourceInventory` in the same file relies on `from __future__ import annotations` + Pydantic v2's lazy resolution — which works but is non-obvious. A handoff item "Pydantic v2 resolves the forward ref lazily at instance construction; if it fails, add `DataRequest.model_rebuild()` at end of module" would have preempted my "will this work?" uncertainty. -0.0 (worked first try; worst case was one failed test run). Cumulative: -0.5, so 9.5/10.
- **What was wrong:** Nothing factually wrong. Pre-commit state matched exactly. Plan §6.3 precedence rules matched the implementation. The "additive-only" claim held through full test suite.
- **ROI:** ~6× on the handoff package. The plan + Phase 1 contract + Phase 2 producer + Session 59's forecast gotchas turned Phase 3's implementation into a focused ~2.5-hour session (vs. an estimated 5+ hours if I had to derive prompt-structure / truncation / sanitization / precedence rules from the requirement alone).

### Phase 3B: Self-assess — 9.5/10

- **Research before creative work:** Yes. SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER + SESSION_NOTES ACTIVE TASK + Session 59 handoff + 20 gotchas read before any action. Plan §6 + §9 Phase 3 + 12 source/test files loaded in one parallel batch before any Edit.
- **Stakeholder corrections:** 0. Operator's "work on backlog item 1. Data source inventory — Phase 3" was unambiguous; plan §9 Phase 3 gave the scope; no re-scopes or re-clarifications needed.
- **What I got right:** (a) **Additive-only design.** Every pre-existing test passed unmodified after the schema changes landed (verified before adding any Phase 3 tests). The `| None = None` + `default_factory=list` defaults honored the plan's backward-compat invariant perfectly. (b) **`_dump_request` inventory deduplication** — caught independently that serializing the full `DataRequest` JSON would render the inventory twice in the prompt; fixed with `payload.pop("data_source_inventory", None)` and a pinning test. Token-budget savings + prompt-consistency win. (c) **Hasattr-style parser compat for `inventory_entries_used`.** `item.get("inventory_entries_used", [])` rather than `item["inventory_entries_used"]` — pre-Phase-3 fixtures + third-party clients that don't return the key still parse. Also caught the subtle issue that `_FakeCLIClient` deliberately omits the field and still produces a valid `PrimaryQuerySpec` (default_factory handles it). (d) **Two-layer sanitization.** Control chars first (plan §9 Phase 3 gotcha #2), then length bound (§12 Q5 default). Each tested separately. (e) **Truncation at MAX_INVENTORY_ENTRIES_IN_PROMPT=20 vs token budget.** Picked 20 as a reasonable default without deliberation; pinned with a test that ~27-entry inventory surfaces "and 7 more sources truncated". Easily tunable later if a pilot surfaces a different sweet spot. (f) **Pre-Phase-3 JSON blob compat.** Two new regression tests pin that legacy `DataRequest` + `PrimaryQuery` blobs (without the new fields) continue to validate — catches any future Pydantic-strict drift. (g) **Integration test with InventoryAwareFake.** End-to-end plumbing pinned without a live LLM — the test captures `data_source_inventory` as it enters the LLM and asserts `report.primary_queries[0].inventory_entries_used` equals the seeded value. (h) **Learning #29 24th consecutive clean, Learning #34 13th post-promotion clean, Learning #38 7th-instance (PROMOTION), Learning #39 6th-instance (PROMOTION).** Promotion thresholds reached for Learning #38 + Learning #39.
- **What I got wrong:** (a) **First test run failed with "unexpected keyword argument `data_source_inventory`" on FakeLLMClient + ExplodingLLM in test_data_agent.py.** I added the kwarg to `_FakeCLIClient` in cli.py but forgot the two test-local fake clients. Caught in ~30 seconds by pytest. -0.25. (b) **Three test-class setup issues after the initial grep sweep.** `_inventory()` helper defaulted `producer_id="test_producer_v1"` but `_sample_entries()` used `"information_schema_probe_v1"` → DataSourceInventory cross-field validator rejected the mismatch. Fixed by aligning producer_id across helpers. ~2 min iteration. -0.0 (fast recovery, caught mechanically). (c) **Two inventory-block-absent assertions were too strict.** `assert "Available data sources" not in user_msg` fired false-positive because the JSON instructions mention "Available data sources block". Fixed by asserting against the block TITLE (`"Available data sources (prefer"`) rather than the phrase. ~1 min. -0.0 (cosmetic). (d) **One `F541` f-string-without-placeholder** in a test assertion — `f"and 5 more sources truncated"` had the leading `f` but no substitution. Caught by ruff, fixed in < 30 sec. -0.0. Cumulative: -0.5, so 9.5/10.
- **Quality bar vs previous sessions:** Comparable to Session 59 (Phase 2 producer) and Session 58 (Phase 1 contract) — the three phases together form a coherent architectural implementation arc. Phase 3 is the consumer side so it had the most plumbing touch-points, but additive-only discipline + 32 new tests kept the change bounded. Slightly above Session 59 on close-out-ROI (the handoff was good enough that 2.5h was realistic; Session 58's 20 gotchas and plan quality compound here).

### Phase 3C: Learnings

**Learning #29 grep-before-insert — 24th consecutive clean validation.** Session 60 zero heading-duplication fumbles. Running count: 24. Institutional reliability confirmed.

**Learning #34 parallel-Read-before-parallel-Edit — 13th consecutive post-promotion validation.** One 14-call batch up front; zero "File has not been read yet" errors across ~25 Edits. Running count: 13 post-promotion.

**Learning #38 read filing-session provenance — 7th instance → PROMOTE.** Session 60 grounded against Session 57 (plan) + Session 58 (contract) + Session 59 (producer). All three filing-session references converted to implementation without re-derivation. **Promote to Learning #38 (permanent) — threshold reached.**

**Learning #39 grep-inventory-derived prose numbers re-verified at write time — 6th instance → PROMOTE.** CHANGELOG Verified bullet, Self-assess percentages, Gotchas pre-commit state all sourced from fresh `uv run pytest -q 2>&1 | tail -5` output at close-out, not memory. Running count: 6 instances. **Promote to Learning #39 (permanent) — threshold reached.**

**Candidate Learning #40 (sibling-package pattern before prompt edits).** 1st instance (Session 56) — applicable this session (new prompt block + new parser kwarg for `generate_primary_queries`). Mechanical check applied: I re-read `AnthropicLLMClient.generate_primary_queries` structure (system prompt + user prompt + `_call_claude` + `_extract_json`) BEFORE extending; the extensions landed in the same pattern. 2nd instance confirmed. Carry forward; 1 more instance needed for promotion.

**Candidate Learning #41 (shared-abstraction check for adjacent BACKLOG items).** 2nd instance confirmed — Phase 3 consumes what Phase 1 (contract) + Phase 2 (producer) produce. Abstraction check is trivially satisfied by the plan document itself. Carry forward; 1 more instance needed.

**Candidate Learning #42 (cross-field validator error messages name both offenders + known-valid set).** No new Pydantic validators added this session; Phase 3 only consumes Phase 1's validator. 1st instance (Session 58) still stands. Carry forward.

**Candidate Learning #43 (SQLAlchemy Inspector for dialect-agnostic reflection).** Not applicable this session — no reflection work. 1st instance (Session 59) still stands. Carry forward.

**Candidate Learning #44 (hasattr-dispatch for optional Protocol methods).** 1st instance (Session 59) — Phase 3 DID extend the Protocol (`data_source_inventory` kwarg on `generate_primary_queries`) rather than hasattr-dispatch. Is this a counter-example? No: the Protocol extension is correct because EVERY LLM client now needs to accept the kwarg (core contract), even if they ignore it. Hasattr-dispatch would be correct if some clients wanted to emit "I don't handle inventory" loudly — they don't, they just ignore. The decision rule from Learning #44 holds: core → extend Protocol; optional per-implementation → hasattr-dispatch. 1st instance still stands.

**Candidate Learning #45 (NEW this session): dedupe inline-data when a request object is both rendered raw AND re-rendered in a specialized block.** When a prompt renders a request twice (once as raw JSON dump, once as a curated/prompt-friendly block), the specialized block wins on clarity + token efficiency. The raw dump should strip the now-redundant field. Otherwise the LLM sees two potentially inconsistent representations and may pick either — especially bad when the specialized block includes truncation the raw dump does not. **Mechanical:** in prompt builders that render the same structured data twice, explicitly remove the field from the raw-dump side (e.g. `payload.pop("foo", None)`) and pin the removal with a test. **1st instance:** Session 60 (`_dump_request` strips `data_source_inventory` because `_build_inventory_block` renders it with truncation + sanitization). Carry forward; 2 more instances needed for promotion.

**Candidate Learning #46 (NEW this session): when extending a Protocol, audit EVERY fake/stub/subclass.** Protocol extensions touch three classes of consumers: (1) production impls (expected — one site in `anthropic_client.py`), (2) production fakes (expected — `_FakeCLIClient` in `cli.py`), (3) **test fakes + their subclasses** (easy to miss — `FakeLLMClient` + `ExplodingLLM` in `test_data_agent.py`). Class 3 is the common failure mode because tests are "peripheral" in mental models but the Protocol signature constraint is equally binding. **Mechanical:** before any Protocol extension, grep for `def <method_name>` across test files and update every match, including subclasses that override. **1st instance:** Session 60 (FakeLLMClient + ExplodingLLM both needed the new kwarg; first test run failed; caught in 30s). Carry forward; 2 more instances needed for promotion.

### Session 58 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 59)

**Score: 9.5/10.** Session 58's handoff was highly actionable. The 20 gotchas + Session 58's forecast of Phase 2 scope converted mechanically into Phase 0 orientation + Phase 2 implementation. The plan document (Session 57) + the contract schema (Session 58) together gave me everything I needed to implement Phase 2 without any re-scoping round-trip with the operator. Two gotchas stand out as highest-value: gotcha #3 (Learning #29 grep pattern for Session 59's stub) converted directly into a zero-fumble Phase 1B stub, and gotcha #4 (Learning #34 parallel-Read recipe naming specific files) matched the Phase 2 surface exactly — one 10-call parallel batch covered all context.

- **What helped:** (a) **Gotcha #1 pre-commit state** (pytest 509/509 @ 97.25%) — matched exactly at Phase 0 baseline. Post-Phase-2: 537 @ 97.22% (+28 tests, -0.03% coverage — in line with expectations for a producer module + tests). (b) **Gotcha #2 BACKLOG count** (5 `[ ]`, 0 `[x]`) + Learning #26 Session #29 of application — Phase 0 baseline matched; close-out removed 1 line per convention. (c) **Gotcha #3 Learning #29 grep-before-insert — 23rd consecutive validation.** 11 hits, all pre-existing forward references in Session 58's handoff. Zero fumble on Phase 1B stub. (d) **Gotcha #4 parallel-Read recipe** — 10-call parallel batch (plan §5.2 + §9 Phase 2 + db.py + cli.py + llm.py + anthropic_client.py + agent.py + schemas.py + __init__.py + test glob + sample fixture) covered all context in one shot; zero "File has not been read yet" errors on 15+ subsequent Edits. (e) **Gotcha #5 plan §5.2 as authoritative signature** — `probe_information_schema(db, *, include_schemas, llm, request_context) -> DataSourceInventory` matched plan §5.2 sketch verbatim; zero signature drift between plan and implementation. (f) **Gotcha #11 cross-field validator blast radius** — Phase 2 builds `ProducerMetadata` first, then entries, then `DataSourceInventory` in one construction call; zero partial-state rejection. (g) **Gotcha #13 `information_schema` dialect coverage** — SQLAlchemy's `Inspector` turned the "PostgreSQL + SQLite" scope into a transparent win: the same code handles both dialects without a dialect switch. (h) **Gotcha #14 `--rank-with-llm` OPTIONAL** — I opted IN to the ranking path rather than deferring; scope pressure was manageable (~80 LOC across anthropic client + llm dataclass + fake stub + CLI flag). (i) **Gotcha #15 decoupling guarantee** — zero `IntakeReport` import in `discovery.py`; the decoupling test auto-verified green in the full suite. (j) **Gotcha #16 CHANGELOG 26-consecutive-structure match** — extending to 27 on autopilot. (k) **Gotcha #10 sample fixture reference shape** — used it as a mental reference for what a valid curated inventory looks like when designing the automated inventory shape; Phase 2's inventory JSON mirrors the fixture's structure (entries + producers + created_at + request_context). (l) **Learning #34 parallel-Read-before-parallel-Edit — 12th consecutive post-promotion validation.** Two parallel batches covered all context; zero read-before-edit errors across 15+ Edits.
- **What was missing:** (a) **No explicit hint that `sa.Inspector` is the natural API for dialect-agnostic reflection.** Session 58's gotcha #13 framed the dialect-coverage concern as "PostgreSQL + SQLite target; other dialects fall back to NotImplementedError or empty-entries inventory." I independently discovered `sa.inspect(engine)` gives dialect-agnostic behavior for free (PostgreSQL, SQLite, MySQL, most supported dialects all work through the same API), so the "fall back" branch became degenerate — the inspector handles everything that SQLAlchemy supports, and only truly unsupported dialects raise. A Session 58 hint "check if SQLAlchemy's Inspector API handles the dialect split for you" would have saved ~5 minutes of dialect-switch deliberation. -0.25 (caught independently; trivial cost). (b) **Plan §5.2 signature had the `llm: LLMClient | None = None` type annotation but did NOT flag the hasattr-dispatch vs Protocol-add tradeoff.** I chose hasattr-dispatch to keep `LLMClient` Protocol unchanged (query-generation-only clients stay valid), but Session 58's handoff could have named this decision upfront (gotcha #14 mentions the fake client should grow a stub if the method is added — which implies a decision point was there). -0.25 (caught independently). (c) **No hint about the mypy TypedDict trap when catching `NoSuchTableError` and assigning `pk_info = {}`.** I hit one mypy error (`Missing keys ("constrained_columns", "name") for TypedDict "ReflectedPrimaryKeyConstraint"`) and restructured to extract `pk_columns` inside the try-block rather than the `pk_info` dict. Five-line fix. A Session 58 gotcha "SQLAlchemy's reflection return types are TypedDicts in mypy-strict; don't assign `{}` as fallback" would have avoided one mypy cycle. -0.0 (trivial; took < 30 seconds to restructure).
- **What was wrong:** Nothing factually wrong. Pre-commit state matched exactly. Gotcha #13's "PostgreSQL + SQLite" scope was if anything under-promised — SQLAlchemy's inspector gives broader coverage for free. The `--rank-with-llm` "OPTIONAL" framing (gotcha #14) was correct; I exercised the option and scope held.
- **ROI:** ~7× on the handoff package. The plan + Phase 1 contract + Session 58's forecast gotchas converted Phase 2's implementation into a ~2-hour focused session instead of a multi-hour design + implementation bundle. Without the plan and the contract, Phase 2 would have deliberated (a) what fields the inventory has, (b) what ProducerMetadata looks like, (c) where to put the discovery module, (d) what CLI flags to expose — each 10–15 minutes. The gotcha list pre-answered all of these.

5. **`ReadOnlyDB.get_information_schema` (`db.py`).** Delegates to `sqlalchemy.inspect(self._engine)` for dialect-agnostic reflection. System schemas (`information_schema`, `pg_catalog`) skipped by default; `schemas=[...]` filter for explicit allow-list. Returns one dict per table/view with `namespace`, `name`, `entity_kind` ("table" or "view"), `columns` (list of per-column dicts), and `primary_key_columns`. Static helper `_reflect_entity` handles per-table introspection (columns + PK + FK) with `NoSuchTableError` caught for tables that vanish between discovery and reflection. +~60 lines.

6. **`probe_information_schema` (`discovery.py`, new module).** Public-API signature verbatim from plan §5.2. Builds `DataSourceEntry` per table with full `ColumnMetadata`, PK/FK flags, and FK targets. Degradation path catches `(SQLAlchemyError, NotImplementedError, RuntimeError)` and returns an empty-entries inventory whose single producer's `notes` names the error — so downstream consumers can distinguish "no tables" from "probe failed" without inspecting return types. Optional LLM ranking via `hasattr(llm, "rank_candidate_tables")` — when present, returned `TableRanking` dicts applied via `model_copy(update={...})` to preserve Pydantic's cross-field validator invariants. 27 SLoC at 100% coverage.

7. **`TableRanking` dataclass (`llm.py`).** New frozen dataclass (`fully_qualified_name`, `relevance_score`, `relevance_reason`). Documented as the output shape for optional `rank_candidate_tables` LLM methods; NOT added to the `LLMClient` Protocol — clients that do query-generation only remain valid.

8. **`AnthropicLLMClient.rank_candidate_tables` (`anthropic_client.py`).** Same system-prompt + user-prompt + `_call_claude` + `_extract_json` pattern as siblings. Prompt bounds per-table column list to first 8 names for token-budget safety. Returns `list[TableRanking]`. +~45 lines. 3 mocked-Claude tests.

9. **`discover` CLI subcommand (`cli.py`).** Flags: `--db-url` (required), `--output` / `-o` (required), `--include-schemas` (repeatable, default `[]` = all non-system schemas), `--rank-with-llm` (default off), `--request-context` (default None), `--model` (default `DEFAULT_MODEL`), `--fake-llm` (default off). Connects via `ReadOnlyDB`, optionally builds an LLM when `--rank-with-llm`, calls `probe_information_schema`, writes result as indented JSON. Exit status 0 + confirmation line on success.

10. **`_FakeCLIClient.rank_candidate_tables` stub (`cli.py`).** Deterministic fake: first entry 0.9, each next 0.1 less (floor 0.0). Enables `--fake-llm --rank-with-llm` end-to-end in CI without a real API key.

11. **Re-exports in `__init__.py`.** Added `probe_information_schema` (from `discovery`) and `TableRanking` (from `llm`) to the top-level package imports and `__all__`. Top-level `from model_project_constructor_data_agent import probe_information_schema` now works.

12. **Tests (3 files).** `tests/data_agent_package/test_discovery.py` (new, 21 tests, ~350 lines) — 6 test classes covering happy path, schema filtering, degradation, round-trip, LLM ranking. `tests/data_agent_package/test_cli.py` (+4 tests + 1 modification) — CLI discover smoke, `--rank-with-llm --fake-llm`, `--include-schemas` filter, unreachable-DB error path. `tests/data_agent_package/test_anthropic_client.py` (+3 tests) — mocked Claude ranking happy path, None `request_context` handling, malformed-response `LLMParseError`.

13. **USAGE.md updates.** New "Example 4 — Data-source discovery (`discover` CLI)" section (~50 lines) with three CLI examples + Python-API example. Public-API block expanded from 10 names to 12 (adds `probe_information_schema`, `TableRanking`) and annotates `ReadOnlyDB` with its new `get_information_schema` method. "Data source inventory" summary updated to reflect Phase 2 shipping.

All numeric claims pasted from live pytest/ruff/mypy output (Learning #39 applied — 5th instance).

Read existing test patterns (`test_inventory_schemas.py` helper style, `test_cli.py` CliRunner pattern, `test_anthropic_client.py` mocked Claude pattern) before drafting Phase 2 tests.
- **Stakeholder corrections:** 0. Operator's "work on Data source inventory — Phase 2" was unambiguous; plan §9 Phase 2 gave the scope; no re-scopes or re-clarifications needed.
- **What I got right:** (a) **Used SQLAlchemy's `Inspector` for dialect-agnostic reflection.** Session 58's gotcha #13 framed "PostgreSQL + SQLite" as the target with other dialects falling back; `sa.inspect(engine)` gives transparent coverage of all supported dialects, turning a potential dialect-switch into a single code path. (b) **Hasattr-dispatched `rank_candidate_tables` rather than extending the `LLMClient` Protocol.** Query-generation-only LLM clients remain valid; future Protocol extension is non-breaking. (c) **Error-path FIRST-class citizen.** `probe_information_schema` degrades to empty-inventory-with-notes rather than raising — downstream consumers can distinguish "no tables" from "probe failed" by inspecting `ProducerMetadata.notes`, not by exception handling. (d) **Exercised the `--rank-with-llm` OPTIONAL path.** Scope pressure manageable (~80 LOC), the path is tested end-to-end via fake-LLM, and real LLM ranking is wired up for production use. Not deferring made the first real Phase 2 ship complete rather than partial. (e) **`_FakeCLIClient.rank_candidate_tables` stub.** Enables CI smoke-test coverage for `--rank-with-llm --fake-llm` without a real API key. Deterministic scoring (0.9, 0.8, 0.7, ..., floor 0.0) is trivially testable. (f) **Tests pin both happy path AND degradation path.** 21 discovery tests cover tables, views, FKs, schema filters, empty DBs, permission-denied, connect-before-probe, LLM ranking (full, partial, none). Not just "does it work" but "does it fail gracefully." (g) **Learning #29 23rd consecutive, Learning #34 12th post-promotion, Learning #38 6th instance, Learning #39 5th instance.** All discipline counters held; Learning #38 and #39 are both now at promotion-ready count (would trigger next session if pattern holds). (h) **Task-tracking discipline.** 7 tasks pending → in_progress → completed; no abandoned.
- **What I got wrong:** (a) **Initial mypy error on `pk_info = {}` fallback.** Assigned `{}` as a fallback for the SQLAlchemy `ReflectedPrimaryKeyConstraint` TypedDict, which mypy-strict rejected. Restructured to extract `pk_columns: list[str]` outside the try-block and populate inside. ~30 seconds. -0.25 (caught by mypy; trivial restructure). (b) **Three initial `E501` line-too-long misses.** discovery.py had a tuple-indexing expression `.get(entry.fully_qualified_name, (None, None))[0]` that was 108 chars; cli.py had a help text at 149 chars; test_anthropic_client.py had a canned JSON string at 113 chars. All three caught by `ruff check`; fixed by splitting across lines. ~1 minute. -0.0 (mechanical; the ruff pass caught them before commit). (c) **Initial coverage dip to 96.82%.** Adding `AnthropicLLMClient.rank_candidate_tables` without mock tests dropped coverage below baseline (still above the 95% floor). Added 3 mocked-Claude tests to recover to 97.22% — essentially flat vs. baseline's 97.25%. -0.0 (recovered in the same session; no handoff debt). Documented in "Unchanged intentionally" as a follow-up option if pilot demand surfaces. -0.0 (scope-appropriate).

Slightly above Session 53 on LLM-integration ROI (Phase 2 ships both the producer AND the fake stub AND the mocked Claude path). On par with Session 58 for close-out completeness and handoff-package reuse ROI. Carry forward; needs 2 more instances before promotion.

**Candidate Learning #43 (new this session): prefer SQLAlchemy `Inspector` for dialect-agnostic schema reflection rather than hand-rolling dialect switches.**

> Plan §9 Phase 2 gotcha #2 framed `information_schema` dialect coverage as "PostgreSQL + SQLite first; other dialects fall back." I was primed to write a dialect-switch: `if engine.dialect.name == 'postgresql': ... elif 'sqlite': ... else: raise NotImplementedError`. Instead, `sa.inspect(engine)` returns an `Inspector` whose `.get_schema_names()`, `.get_table_names(schema=...)`, `.get_view_names(schema=...)`, `.get_columns(table, schema=...)`, `.get_pk_constraint(table, schema=...)`, and `.get_foreign_keys(table, schema=...)` methods work identically across PostgreSQL, SQLite, MySQL, Oracle, and most other SQLAlchemy-supported dialects. The dialect differences are hidden inside SQLAlchemy's per-dialect reflection implementations; my code stays single-path. Unsupported dialects raise naturally — the try/except in `probe_information_schema` catches `SQLAlchemyError` and `NotImplementedError` uniformly. The result: 60 lines of code handling every SQLAlchemy-supported dialect instead of 200+ lines with per-dialect branches.
> **Mechanical:** any task that reads "query information_schema for X" should first check whether `sa.inspect(engine)` provides the X — it probably does. Dialect-agnostic reflection (schemas, tables, views, columns, PKs, FKs, indexes, table comments, column comments) is all `Inspector`-exposed.
> **Not applicable when:** (a) the data needed is dialect-specific and not in the `Inspector` API (table bloat metrics, query-log miners, vendor-specific metadata like PostgreSQL's `pg_stat_user_tables`), OR (b) SQLAlchemy is not the ORM layer in use. In those cases, a hand-rolled dialect switch is justified.
> **1st instance:** Session 59 (`ReadOnlyDB.get_information_schema` uses `sa.inspect` rather than a dialect switch). Carry forward; needs 2 more instances before promotion.

**Candidate Learning #44 (new this session): use hasattr-dispatch for optional Protocol methods rather than extending the Protocol with required methods.**

> I needed `probe_information_schema` to optionally invoke `rank_candidate_tables` on the passed-in LLM. Two options: (a) extend `LLMClient` Protocol with `rank_candidate_tables` (strongly typed; but breaks query-generation-only clients), OR (b) hasattr-dispatch (weaker typing; but preserves backward compatibility for every existing LLMClient). I chose (b). Reasoning: (1) the ranking path is genuinely optional — a client that only does query generation is still useful; (2) adding to Protocol is non-breaking if every existing client has the method, but we have two implementations (`AnthropicLLMClient`, `_FakeCLIClient`) and adding to both is fine but every FUTURE client would have to add it too; (3) Python's duck-typing supports this naturally via `hasattr`. The cost of (b) is weaker static typing — mypy doesn't see `rank_candidate_tables` as a guaranteed method. The benefit is that a future `OpenAILLMClient` or `BedrockLLMClient` that only implements query generation is still valid without Protocol gymnastics.
> **Mechanical:** when a new method on an existing Protocol is optional (some clients will never need it), use hasattr-dispatch at the call site rather than Protocol extension. When the method is CORE (every client must implement it), extend the Protocol.
> **Not applicable when:** the method is part of the core contract — every implementation of the Protocol must provide it. In that case, Protocol extension is correct; hasattr is a code smell.
> **1st instance:** Session 59 (`rank_candidate_tables` optional on `LLMClient`; hasattr-dispatched by `probe_information_schema`). Carry forward; needs 2 more instances before promotion.

Important considerations for Session 60:

1. **Read plan §6 (consumer integration) + §9 Phase 3 (scope + completion criteria + forecast gotchas) end-to-end BEFORE first Edit.** Plan §6.2 describes the prompt-effect when inventory is present; §6.3 pins the precedence rules with `database_hint`; §6.4 flags the audit/observability decision point (probably `PrimaryQuery.inventory_entries_used`). Read §11 for the cross-phase invariant: inventory-populated request produces SQL that references at least one table from the inventory.

2. **Phase 1 + Phase 2 are locked.** You can import and USE `DataSourceInventory`, `DataSourceEntry`, `ProducerMetadata`, `ColumnMetadata`, and `probe_information_schema` from `model_project_constructor_data_agent`. Every Phase 1 field is tested (33 tests in `test_inventory_schemas.py`); every Phase 2 path is tested (28 tests across `test_discovery.py`, `test_cli.py`, `test_anthropic_client.py`). Phase 3 consumer code MUST NOT modify either — consumer integration is a forward-compatible extension.

3. **`DataRequest.data_source_inventory` addition is additive-only.** Add `| None = None` default so every existing fixture and call site continues to work. 49 files reference `DataRequest` per plan §8.1 — none need changes if the field is optional with a None default. Re-run the full suite after adding the field; every pre-existing test should still pass without modification.

4. **Prompt truncation is load-bearing.** `AnthropicLLMClient` has `DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS = 4096` (`anthropic_client.py:49`). A large inventory (100+ tables) could blow the prompt budget. Plan §9 Phase 3 gotcha #1 names the issue; implementation strategy is "rank by `relevance_score` (if present), emit top-N entries, append `... and M more sources truncated` note." Decide N during implementation (plan leaves it open). Sanitization: strip control chars + bound per-field length (plan §9 Phase 3 gotcha #2; defaults in plan §12 Q5: 2000 chars on `description` / `relevance_reason`).

5. **Precedence rules (plan §6.3):** `database_hint=None, inventory=None` → current behavior; `database_hint=set, inventory=None` → current behavior; `database_hint=None, inventory=set` → LLM uses inventory; `database_hint=set, inventory=set` → LLM uses inventory (richer), hint passed as context; `inventory=[] (empty entries)` → treat identically to `inventory=None`. Phase 3 pins these in the prompt builder AND in a test.

6. **Status-decision invariant (Learning #21).** `DataAgent.run()` at `agent.py:55-61` routes to COMPLETE / INCOMPLETE_REQUEST / EXECUTION_FAILED based on final state. Adding inventory MUST NOT introduce a new terminal status or change the decision criteria. Phase 3 verification pins this explicitly (plan §11).

7. **Provenance field shape (Q6 in plan §12).** Probably `PrimaryQuery.inventory_entries_used: list[str]` (list of fully-qualified names the query references). Phase 3 decides the final shape — could alternatively be a field on `DataReport.summary` or a map in the datasheet. Pick one and pin it in a test.

8. **Phase 3 gotchas list on plan §9 Phase 3 names: MAX_TOKENS headroom, prompt sanitization, backward-compat preservation.** Read those carefully before scoping.

### Session 57 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 58)

**Score: 9.5/10.** Session 57's handoff was highly actionable. The 20 gotchas + plan §4 + §9 Phase 1 end-to-end conversion to code took ~90 minutes of focused work with zero re-reads forced by handoff omissions. The plan document itself carried most of the load — gotchas #1, #3, #4, #5, #6, #8, #9, #11, #12, #13, #14 all converted directly to implementation actions (schema field list, cross-field validator shape, test coverage list, decoupling guarantee call-out, `extra: dict[str, Any]` subtle-field test, re-export pattern, StrictBase duplication rationale, consecutive-validation counters). - **What helped:** (a) **Gotcha #1 pre-commit state** (pytest 476/476 @ 97.19%) — matched exactly at Phase 0 baseline; +33 new tests landed cleanly as predicted. (b) **Gotcha #2 BACKLOG count** (6 `[ ]`, 0 `[x]`) + Learning #26 Session #28 of application — Phase 0 baseline matched; close-out removed 1 line per convention. (c) **Gotcha #4 parallel-Read recipe** — prescriptive list (`schemas.py` + both `__init__.py` + `src/.../schemas/v1/data.py` + test file + data_agent_package/ listing + plan §4/§9 Phase 1) matched the actual Phase 1 surface exactly; single parallel batch landed all context. (d) **Gotcha #5 read plan end-to-end** — plan §4 field-by-field rationale + §9 Phase 1 scope + Phase 1 forecast gotchas all drove concrete code choices (e.g., gotcha #12 "extra: dict[str, Any] is load-bearing AND subtle" → `test_extra_field_accepts_producer_specific_keys` + `test_top_level_extra_keys_still_forbidden`). (e) **Gotcha #10 pre-answered Q1/Q8/Q9/Q10** — zero deliberation round-trip; Q1 `float | None`, Q8 JSON-only (no YAML code), Q9 Literal-pinned, Q10 free text. All four landed per defaults. (f) **Gotcha #11 decoupling guarantee** — zero `IntakeReport` import needed in Phase 1; decoupling test auto-verified green post-suite. (g) **Gotcha #13 re-export pattern** — mirrored `DataRequest` / `DataReport` shape exactly in all three re-export sites + `__all__` lists. (h) **Gotcha #14 StrictBase duplication** — used the data-agent-package StrictBase for all four types; zero coupling-cross. (i) **Gotcha #6 new test file over extending `test_data.py`** — `tests/data_agent_package/test_inventory_schemas.py` matches the sibling convention and keeps schema tests local to the data-agent package. (j) **Plan §3.4 precedence (inventory wins when both present)** — Phase 3 concern, but the rationale let me confidently write "no Phase 3 work" today. (k) **Learning #29 grep-before-insert — 22nd consecutive clean validation.** 15 hits, all pre-existing forward references in Session 57's handoff. Zero fumble on Phase 1B stub. (l) **Learning #34 parallel-Read-before-parallel-Edit — 11th consecutive post-promotion validation.** One 9-call parallel batch (plan + schemas.py + 2 __init__.py + data.py + test_data.py + decoupling test + data_agent_package listing + fixtures listing + memory file) covered all context; zero "File has not been read yet" errors on 6 subsequent Edits. (m) **Gotcha #15 CHANGELOG 25-consecutive-structure match** — extending to 26 on autopilot.
- **What was missing:** (a) **Plan §4 mentioned `extra: dict[str, Any]` but did NOT flag that `extra` is a potential reserved name in Pydantic v2.** I independently verified `extra` is safe as a field name (it is a `ConfigDict` option but not a reserved attribute), but a line in Session 57's gotcha #12 saying "verified `extra` is safe as a field name in Pydantic v2" would have saved ~2 minutes of caution. -0.25 (cosmetic; caught independently). (b) **No explicit hint about whether the sample curated fixture should be registered anywhere for future phases.** I chose to add a `TestSampleCuratedFixture::test_fixture_validates` regression test to prevent fixture drift — this is the right call for a JSON fixture that Phase 2/3 will rely on, but Session 57's plan §9 Phase 1 step 5 said only "recommended" without naming the drift-protection mechanism. -0.0 (caught; right call made independently). (c) **Ruff `I001` import-ordering autofix required a second pass.** Inserting new imports in alphabetical-by-line order did not match ruff's expected shape (ruff put `DataSourceEntry` / `DataSourceInventory` AFTER `Datasheet` because uppercase-D-s comes alphabetically before lowercase-r but the per-letter sort is case-sensitive). One `--fix` pass cleaned it up; no behavioral change. A Session 57 gotcha "expect one ruff `--fix` pass on the re-export edits" would have saved a ~30 second cycle. -0.0 (trivial; autofix was fast).

### Phase 3B: Self-assess — 9.5/10

- **Research before creative work:** Yes. Read SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER + SESSION_NOTES ACTIVE TASK + Session 57 handoff + all 20 gotchas before any action. Parallel-Read the 9 context files including plan §4 + §9 Phase 1 before any Edit. Verified via smoke-test `uv run python -c "from model_project_constructor_data_agent import DataSourceInventory..."` that imports work before writing tests. Read existing test patterns (`tests/schemas/fixtures.py` helper style, `test_data.py` test-class shape) before drafting new tests.
- **Stakeholder corrections:** 0. Operator's "work on BACKLOG item 1" was unambiguous; plan §9 Phase 1 gave the scope; no re-scopes or re-clarifications needed.
- **What I got right:** (a) **Followed the plan's refinement discretion (gotcha #9) without over-exercising it.** Kept all field names from plan §4 verbatim; no cosmetic churn. The only "refinement" was adding a `TestSampleCuratedFixture::test_fixture_validates` drift-protection test — a one-test addition that didn't require plan updates. (b) **Used the data-agent-package `StrictBase` for all four new types** per gotcha #14; zero coupling to the main-package `StrictBase`. (c) **Cross-field validator error message includes both dangling and known producer_ids.** Enables debugging without a second validation round-trip; future Phase 2 producers will surface dangling-id bugs with one clear message. (d) **Re-export identity checked in a test** (`test_both_reexport_paths_import_same_class` asserts `DSI_v1 is DataSourceInventory is DSI_v1_data`). Pins the re-export pattern; a future drift that creates a second class is an immediate test failure. (e) **Sample fixture includes a FK relationship** (`subrogation_outcomes.claim_id` → `claim_events.claim_id`). Not strictly required by plan §9 Phase 1 step 5 but lets Phase 2/3 exercise the FK path without constructing a second fixture. (f) **`extra: dict[str, Any]` tested both directions** per gotcha #12 — positive (`test_extra_field_accepts_producer_specific_keys`) + negative (`test_top_level_extra_keys_still_forbidden`). Without both, a future "tightening" of the schema could silently remove the extension point. (g) **Parametrized tests for Literal enforcement** (`test_all_producer_types_accepted`, `test_all_entity_kinds_accepted`). Adding a new Literal value in a future version now requires a parametrize list update AND a test, not just a type annotation change — the test surface tracks the contract surface. (h) **Learning #29 22nd consecutive, Learning #34 11th post-promotion, Learning #39 4th instance.** All discipline counters held. (i) **Task-tracking discipline.** 7 tasks through pending → in_progress → completed; no abandoned tasks.
- **What I got wrong:** (a) **Initial import-ordering in the new `__init__.py` entries was alphabetical-by-eye but not ruff-compliant.** I placed `DataSourceEntry` / `DataSourceInventory` before `Datasheet` (case-insensitive alphabetical feels natural), but ruff sorts case-sensitively — uppercase `D-S` < uppercase `D-a` is false, ruff wants `DataSourceEntry` after `Datasheet`. Caught by `ruff check`; fixed by `ruff --fix`. ~30 second cost. -0.25 (small; autofix resolved it cleanly). (b) **Ran `uv run pytest tests/data_agent_package/test_inventory_schemas.py -q` as a first sanity check and got a misleading "coverage failure" because single-file pytest runs against the 95% gate show 10.86% coverage.** Not a real issue — the full suite recovers to 97.25% — but I should have either used `--no-cov` for the sanity check or skipped directly to the full suite. Trivial. -0.0 (ignored the noise; full suite confirmed green). (c) **One progress-narration check during the session.** No "Let me..." phrasing observed on my scan; Learning #30 compliance holds. -0.0.
- **Quality bar vs previous sessions:** Comparable to Session 36 (retry kwarg implementation), Session 52 (resume demotion), Session 53 (JSON parser tolerance) — all single-file / single-concern implementation sessions whose deliverable is schema or control-flow code with high test coverage. Slightly above average on test-per-class ratio (9 tests for `DataSourceEntry`'s 20 fields is healthy; parametrized Literals cover 10 more in-line). On par with Session 56 for close-out completeness and on par with Session 57 for handoff-package reuse ROI. Net self-score: **9.5/10** (deduction: -0.25 ruff import-ordering miss + -0.25 one cycle burned on single-file-pytest coverage noise).

### Phase 3C: Learnings

**Learning #29 grep-before-insert — 22nd consecutive clean validation.** No heading-duplication fumble in Session 58. Running count: 22. Institutional reliability confirmed.

**Learning #34 parallel-Read-before-parallel-Edit — 11th consecutive post-promotion validation.** One 9-call batch, 6 subsequent Edits, zero "File has not been read yet" errors. Running count: 11 post-promotion. The habit is load-bearing; mechanically reliable.

**Learning #38 read filing-session provenance — 5th-instance validation.** Session 58's grounding was Session 57's plan (the filing session). Plan §4 field-by-field rationale + §9 Phase 1 scope + gotchas list all converted to code decisions without re-derivation. Running count: 5 instances. Candidate for PROMOTION next session if it holds.

**Learning #39 grep-inventory-derived prose numbers re-verified at write time — 4th-instance validation.** CHANGELOG Verified bullet cites fresh pytest (509/509 @ 97.25%) / ruff / mypy output. No memory-based numbers. Running count: 4 instances. Candidate for PROMOTION next session if it holds.

**Candidate Learning #40 (sibling-package pattern before prompt edits) — no instance this session.** Not a prompt-edit session. 1st instance still stands (Session 56). Carry forward.

**Candidate Learning #41 (shared-abstraction check for adjacent BACKLOG items) — no concrete instance this session.** Session 58's BACKLOG item is Phase 1 of a plan Session 57 already framed as an architectural contract — the shared abstraction is explicit. The learning's mechanical test (do adjacent items produce / consume the same data shape?) is satisfied by design. No new instance to log; 1st instance (Session 57) still stands. Carry forward.

**Candidate Learning #42 (new this session): when implementing a Pydantic cross-field validator, raise from `@model_validator(mode="after")` with BOTH the offending values AND the set of known-valid values in the error message.**

> The data-source-inventory validator checks that every `DataSourceEntry.producer_id` resolves to a `ProducerMetadata.producer_id`. Simplest form: `if dangling: raise ValueError(f"dangling: {dangling}")`. Richer form (chosen): `raise ValueError(f"... do not resolve ...: {dangling}. Known producers: {sorted(known)}.")`. The cost is trivial (one f-string computation) and the benefit is concrete: when a Phase 2 producer later emits an inventory where `producer_id` has a typo (`information_schema_probe_v2` vs the declared `_v1`), the error message names both the typo AND the correct value in a single line. Future debuggers see "expected one of {v1}, got {v2}" not "dangling: {v2}" and then need a second grep to see what producers are declared.
> **Mechanical:** every `@model_validator` that raises on a cross-field constraint should include the set/list of valid options in the error message when the valid set is known and small. Single-offender shape: `ValueError(f"{field} {value} must be one of {sorted(valid)}")`. Multi-offender shape: `ValueError(f"{field} values {offenders} not in {sorted(valid)}")`.
> **Not applicable when** the valid set is large / unbounded (e.g., validating that a datetime is later than another — there's no "known-valid set" to print) OR when the valid set leaks sensitive data (e.g., user IDs). In those cases, the simple shape is correct.
> **1st instance:** Session 58 (`DataSourceInventory._producer_ids_resolve`). Carry forward; needs 2 more instances before promotion.

**Existing learnings load-bearing this session:**
- **Learning #11** (trust code over plan for signatures): N/A this session — plan §4 signatures matched Pydantic v2 conventions without adjustment.
- **Learning #18** (planning-to-implementation boundaries): Applied. ONE phase implemented. Did NOT start Phase 2 (information_schema producer) despite having a clear design in mind from plan §5.2. Phase 2 is Session 59's scope.

### Phase 3D: Handoff to Session 59

**Next deliverable (per operator's numerical-order directive + plan §9 Phase 2):**

**BACKLOG item 1 — Data source inventory Phase 2: `information_schema` reference producer.** Add `ReadOnlyDB.get_information_schema(schemas: list[str] | None = None) -> list[dict]` method in `packages/data-agent/.../db.py`. Add `probe_information_schema(db, ...) -> DataSourceInventory` function in a new `packages/data-agent/.../discovery.py` module. Add `discover` CLI subcommand in `packages/data-agent/.../cli.py` with flags `--db-url`, `--output`, `--include-schemas`, `--rank-with-llm`, `--fake-llm`. Fake-DB / fake-LLM coverage only; live-DB deferred. Plan §9 Phase 2 pins scope + completion criteria + forecast gotchas. Probably 1-2 sessions depending on `--rank-with-llm` LLM method scope.

Important considerations for Session 59:

1. **Read plan §5.2 (producer public-API sketch) + §9 Phase 2 (scope + completion criteria + forecast gotchas) end-to-end BEFORE first Edit.** The plan's public-API sketch for `probe_information_schema` is prescriptive about the signature (`db, *, include_schemas, llm, request_context`); follow it unless you find a concrete reason to deviate.

2. **Phase 1 contract schema is locked. You can import and USE `DataSourceInventory` / `DataSourceEntry` / `ProducerMetadata` / `ColumnMetadata` from `model_project_constructor_data_agent`.** Every field is tested (33 tests in `tests/data_agent_package/test_inventory_schemas.py`). The cross-field validator rejects dangling `producer_id`s — Phase 2 must construct `ProducerMetadata` BEFORE building entries, else the validator fails.

3. **The sample curated fixture (`tests/fixtures/sample_curated_inventory.json`) is a valid reference shape.** 2 entries, 1 producer, full column metadata including a FK. Phase 2 can use it as a comparison target for what a valid `DataSourceInventory` JSON looks like, but the fixture itself is for Phase 2/3 consumers — Phase 2's producer will emit its OWN inventory, not parse this one.

4. **`information_schema` dialect differences.** Per gotcha #2 of plan §9 Phase 2: target PostgreSQL + SQLite first; other dialects (MySQL / BigQuery / Snowflake) fall back to `NotImplementedError` or an empty-entries inventory with a `ProducerMetadata.notes` field naming the unsupported dialect. SQLite exposes `sqlite_master` rather than `information_schema` — the probe may need a dialect-switch; confirm with `db.url.drivername` or similar.

5. **The `--rank-with-llm` flag is OPTIONAL for Phase 2 MVP.** If LLM ranking adds scope pressure, DEFER it — ranking without LLM is valid (`relevance_score=None`). Phase 2 closure does NOT require the ranking path if all other criteria are green.

6. **Cross-field validator blast radius.** The `DataSourceInventory._producer_ids_resolve` validator fires AT CONSTRUCTION. If Phase 2's producer builds entries incrementally and assembles the inventory at the end, the validator runs once at the end. If Phase 2 tries to stream entries into a partial inventory (not recommended), the validator may reject intermediate states. Build complete inventory in one `DataSourceInventory(...)` call.

7. **`extra: dict[str, Any]` is a deliberate extension point for the `information_schema` probe.** Phase 2 can include PostgreSQL-specific metadata here (tablespace, oid, replica status) without polluting the core schema. Test that the probe's output passes round-trip through `model_dump_json` → `model_validate_json`.

8. **Phase 2 gotchas list on plan §9 Phase 2 names: `information_schema` dialects, `rank_candidate_tables` LLM method, `--fake-llm` flag.** Read those carefully before scoping.

9. **Decoupling guarantee still applies.** Phase 2 stays inside `packages/data-agent/`; no `IntakeReport` imports. `tests/test_data_agent_decoupling.py` verifies.

10. **`tests/data_agent_package/test_inventory_schemas.py` tests should remain UNCHANGED by Phase 2.** They pin the contract; Phase 2 is a producer implementation. If Phase 2 needs to modify any of those tests, that's a signal the contract is changing — stop and re-open Phase 1 instead of editing tests.

11. **Memory `feedback_data_source_discovery.md` auto-loads in Session 59's context.** It reaffirms the architectural invariants; if Phase 2 is tempted to embed discovery inside `DataAgent.run()`, the memory is the citation for pushing back.

**Future deliverables (numerical order continuing):**
- **Item 2 (after Phase 2):** Data source inventory Phase 3 — consumer integration. One session, biggest Phase.
- **Item 3 (optional):** Data source inventory Phase 4 — intake coupling. Deferred unless pilot demands.
- **Item 4:** Statistical terminology glossary. One session.
- **Item 5:** Tutorial renderer: migrate to MkDocs. Probably 1-2 sessions.

### Session 56 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 57)

**Score: 9.5/10.** Session 56's handoff was highly actionable. The 16 gotchas converted mechanically into Phase 0 + Phase 1 action, and several pre-answered load-bearing architectural questions that would otherwise have cost me 5–15 minutes of deliberation each. Two gotchas stand out as most valuable — gotcha #11 ("data-agent / intake-agent coupling is not yet wired; default is NOT to wire the coupling this cycle — ship each mode independently and revisit integration after both exist") and gotcha #12 ("live-LLM live-database testing is out of scope for the first session … file as a separate Scope-C-style task"). Both converted directly into plan §5.3 / §9 Phase 4 deferral and §9 Phase 2 fake-DB-only scoping respectively. Without those pre-answers, the plan would have spent time debating whether to bundle intake coupling into Phase 3 (probably landing on "no, but with 200 words of justification") and whether to require live-DB coverage in Phase 2 (probably landing on "no, but spending time on the scope boundary"). Gotcha #3 (Learning #29 grep pattern for Session 57) converted directly into the Phase 1B stub safety check. Gotcha #8 (Learning #39 — grep-inventory numbers re-verified at write time) converted directly into §8 of the plan being built from fresh greps rather than memory. Gotcha #16 (operator numerical-order directive) gave immediate Phase 1 clarity.

- **What helped:** (a) **Gotcha #1 pre-commit state** (pytest 476/476 @ 97.19%) — matched exactly at close-out; zero code this session, zero delta. (b) **Gotcha #2 BACKLOG count** (3 `[ ]`, 0 `[x]`) + Learning #26 Session #27 of application — Phase 0 baseline matched; close-out flipped the BACKLOG shape (1 line → 4 per-phase lines) as a structural supersede, not a shipped-item removal. (c) **Gotcha #11** — see above; highest-value single hint. (d) **Gotcha #12** — see above; second-highest-value. (e) **Gotcha #4 parallel-Read recipe** — prescriptive file list (`agent.py` + `graph.py` + `nodes.py` + `state.py` + `protocol.py` + `anthropic_client.py` + `schemas.py` + `USAGE.md` + tests listing + architecture-plan.md grep) matched the actual data-agent surface exactly. All 10 reads landed with full context in one parallel batch. (f) **Gotcha #7 Learning #38 filing-session-provenance grep** — applied mechanically; confirmed `docs/architecture-history/initial_purpose.txt:88` as the analyst-reusability ground ("This same basic agent would likely be useful for just writing queries in general"). Design ground: the contract extends the reusable-analyst-tool framing to multiple producers. (g) **Gotcha #8 candidate Learning #39 — grep-inventory-derived prose numbers re-verified at write time** — applied preemptively to plan §8. All four grep counts (49 DataRequest files, 14 database_hint files, 7 generate_primary_queries files, 18 ReadOnlyDB files) re-run at plan-write time, not pasted from memory. 3rd-instance validation. (h) **Gotcha #13 CHANGELOG 24-consecutive-match convention** — landed the 25th match on autopilot. (i) **Gotcha #16 operator numerical-order directive** — immediate Phase 1 clarity. (j) **Learning #29 grep-before-insert — 21st consecutive clean validation.** Grep pattern `Session 57|### What Session 57 Did|### Session 56 Handoff Evaluation|Post-Session-57` returned 14 hits, all pre-existing forward references in Session 56's handoff (lines 8, 17, 65, 92, 103, 109, 115, 124, 130, 134, 138, 144, 146, 148, 156, etc.). Zero fumble on the Phase 1B stub. (k) **Learning #34 parallel-Read-before-parallel-Edit — 10th consecutive post-promotion validation.** Two parallel batches — a 10-call batch (agent / graph / nodes / state / schemas / db / anthropic_client / cli / llm / sql_validation + architecture-plan.md grep + SESSION_NOTES.md grep + tests glob) and a 9-call batch (DataRequest / database_hint / generate_primary_queries / ReadOnlyDB / discovery / schema-import / test-data glob / schemas dir glob + architecture-workstream read). Zero "File has not been read yet" errors on 7 subsequent Edits (SESSION_NOTES.md × 2, plan write × 1, CHANGELOG × 1, BACKLOG × 1, memory file write × 1, memory index edit × 1).
- **What was missing:** (a) **No explicit hint that the operator might reshape the scope architecturally.** Session 56's handoff presented BACKLOG item 1 as a well-scoped implementation task ("probably one session, maybe 1.5"). When the operator reshaped it as an architectural plug-in workstream, my Option A recommendation (terminal mode, one session) was stale inside ~5 minutes of stating it. Session 56 could not reasonably have predicted this — the operator's reframe was novel guidance, not a re-statement of prior architecture. -0.25 (acknowledged as unpredictable; noted for future sessions that adjacent-feature BACKLOG items deserve a "shared-abstraction" check during Phase 1). Candidate Learning #41 below. (b) **No explicit hint about the relationship between Session 56's intake probe and the data-agent discovery mode.** Gotcha #11 said "the two sides can potentially couple but default is NO." This was correct but under-developed — a sentence like "both are mechanisms for identifying data sources; consider whether a shared abstraction belongs between them before picking a data-agent-internal implementation" would have surfaced the plug-in question at my Phase 1 rather than after the operator's Phase 1 reshape. -0.25 (cosmetic; the operator caught it anyway; noted for symmetric sessions). (c) **No hint about the decoupling guarantee for the architectural plan.** The plan has to respect `tests/test_data_agent_decoupling.py` (Phase 4 interview-sourced converter lives in orchestrator, not in the data-agent walled garden). I caught this during plan §11 drafting by grep'ing for `test_data_agent_decoupling` on my own; a Session 56 gotcha would have surfaced it to Phase 0. -0.0 (caught; cosmetic).
- **What was wrong:** Nothing factually wrong. Pre-commit state matched exactly. File-list gotcha #4 was accurate. Consecutive-validation counters held. BACKLOG baseline count matched. Operator directive was correctly characterized.
- **ROI:** ~6× on the handoff package. The 16 gotchas + parallel-Read recipe + two pre-answered architectural questions (#11, #12) let me proceed from Phase 0 → operator-confirmed plan direction in ~30 minutes and from there to a committable plan + BACKLOG + CHANGELOG + handoff in another ~90 minutes. The gotcha #11 + #12 pre-answers were the highest-value hints — each saved ~10–15 minutes of deliberation during plan §5.3 / §9 Phase 2 drafting.

   - §3 Five architectural decisions: (3.1) separation of discovery from consumption as an invariant; (3.2) contract is pure data (Pydantic → JSON), Protocol deferred; (3.3) four producer classes with uniform consumer; (3.4) `database_hint` preservation for backward compat; (3.5) inventory optional throughout.
   - §4 Interface-first schema: `DataSourceInventory`, `DataSourceEntry`, `ProducerMetadata`, `ColumnMetadata`. Field-by-field rationale table. Error contract (cross-field validation on producer_id; empty entries treated as None). Versioning strategy (1.x.y tolerance, breaking changes bump major).
   - §5 Producer ecosystem: curated (pure JSON/YAML files, no code), `information_schema` probe (Phase 2 reference impl with public-API sketch), interview-sourced (Phase 4 converter reading IntakeReport qa_pairs), external catalogs (future DataHub / Amundsen / Collibra etc.).
   - §6 Consumer integration: `DataRequest.data_source_inventory` optional field, `generate_primary_queries` prompt extension, precedence matrix (inventory wins when both present; empty entries treated as None), audit/observability hook.
   - §7 Alternatives considered table: 6 alternatives weighed (A-embed-in-run, B-terminal-mode-only, C-separate-package-day-one, D-Protocol-first, E-extend-database_hint, F-Step-2.5-in-pipeline). Each with Pros / Cons / Why rejected.

Each phase has explicit scope numbered list, completion criteria with all 4 gate commands, session boundary marker, and forecast gotchas.
